feed.title: SAP Community - SD (Sales and Distribution)
feed.subtitle: SD (Sales and Distribution) Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Create a Sales Order with reference to Contract in cross Sales Area
entry.content:
Hello Gurus,

I have a requirement wherein user will be creating a Global Contract, and the Sales Orders will be created with reference to the Contract but it will be Cross Sales Area.

Eg. I have created a Quantity Contract  for Amul India where Sales Area is 1000/10/10 and thereafter I will create a Sales Order for Amul Jamnagar with reference to the Contract wherein Amul Jamnagar belongs to 1001/10/10 and the 2nd Sales Order with reference to the Contract where the Customer is Amul Pune and so on.

We are on SAP RISE 2023.

I have tried using the Reference Sales Organisation /Reference Distribution Channel and Reference Division along with Common Distribn Channel and Common Division but nothing works.

I have tried Customer Hierarchy but that is also of no use eg.   1A Hierarchy is Amul India and 2A is Amul Jamnagar and Amul Pune.

Is there any standard solution for this OR what are the enhancements required to achieve this.

Someone please help me on this.

Thanks and Regards.

Saurav Mallick.

entry.title: vl10b is not showing the due list for created STO's
entry.content:
Hii all,

I have created a STO and it successfully got created, but when I took the po number and tried to open that in VL10B Tcode, it is blank. So I have tried multiple orders with different materials but the same problem. Here is the reference image. Please help me.

entry.title: Customer Invoices based on batches from Outbound delivery - Public cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

We have a business requirement (Public Cloud) where we have to create invoices based on the batches from the outbound delivery.

For example, if we have 3 batches for a line item in the outbound delivery. When we want to invoice, it is required to create the first invoice only for first 2 batches and later second invoice to be created for the 3rd batch. User will decide which batch to be invoiced.

Do we have any solution for this requirement? User don't want to create one line item per batch in the outbound delivery as the number of batches will be high for a line item.

Thanks in advance.

With Regards

Vinu Nanjan

entry.title: KSA E invoice CRN number based on the sales Office
entry.content:
Hi I have requirement in KSA E-Invoicing solution, customer wants the CRN should be fetched based on the sales office. As per the current configuration CRN is fetched from the company code and updated in XML.

Now the Customer Registration number should be based on the sales office and reflected in XML (EDOC_COCKPIT)

entry.title: Credit management issue
entry.content:
Due to the implementation of credit management in some company codes, sales orders are getting blocked during credit checks. This results in empty shipping dates and the orders do not appear in the delivery due list, leading to late deliveries.

Additionally, when a released order’s delivery date is changed, the order gets blocked again for credit checks. The requirement is to prevent re-blocking of released orders after delivery date changes.

Please assist me to find the root cause and help me to troubleshoot this issue.

entry.title: Customer Specific Inventory Reservation based on Bill to party
entry.content:
Our client is seeking an SAP solution to implement a customer-based reservation (at the Bill-to party level) for specific products. 

  1. Reserved stock must be tracked separately from unrestricted inventory.
  2. Sales Orders for the designated customer must draw from the reserved stock first.
  3. Once the reserved stock is fully exhausted, any remaining order quantity should automatically be fulfilled from unrestricted stock.
  4. The reserved stock must remain unavailable to all other customers.
  5. The Customer reservation will have the expiry date, after expiry the reserved stock should move back to unrestricted stock.   

How can we achieve this specific allocation and consumption flow in SAP? We are in SAP ECC.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Consignment process and Sales of Non-Stock Item with Order-Specific Procurement(BDN)
entry.content:
Hi,

We are using Public cloud and our business process is that  we procure the products from external supplier using the process Sales of Non-Stock Item with Order-Specific Procurement(BDN). Once the product is received, we want to ship it to customer using customer consignment process as the billing should be done only when the customer consumes it. Till then it will be in customer location as customer consignment stock.

When we checked in the system, it seems we cannot use both the process in the same document. Have you come across this business requirement? If so, how is it handled?

Thanks in advance

With Regards

Vinu Nanjan

entry.title: Error during Portugal Settlement Document Creation – Series Not Registered for Validation Code
entry.content:
System: SAP S/4HANA (Development)

Country: Portugal (PT)

Issue Description:
During the creation of settlement documents for Portugal with digital signature enabled, the system is preventing document posting with an error related to series validation.

Error Message:

Series LPXX is not registered for validation code; posting is not allowed.

Configuration Performed:
The following configurations have been completed:

  1. Number range maintained for Portugal using object GLO_PT_SET.
  2. Number ranges recorded for the digital signature of settlement documents.
  3. The same number range maintained under object W_LFAKTURA.
  4. Country/Region-specific number ranges defined by assigning the same internal number range to country PT.

Observed Behavior:
Despite completing the above configurations, the system throws the validation code error while creating the settlement document and does not allow posting.

Testing Status:
The issue is currently being tested in the development system. Once the issue is resolved and confirmed, the business will proceed with registering the series on the Portuguese Tax Authority portal.

Request:
Kindly advise if any additional configuration or prerequisite is required to resolve this error, or confirm whether series registration and validation code maintenance is mandatory even for development/testing environments.

Thanks and Regards,

Pankaj

entry.title: Message no. VT806 The cost center is not defined
entry.content: I am trying to reassign a cost center determination to one cost center to another, but I am receiving an error message that the cost center is not defined. The cost center is in the standard hierarchy and active. What is causing this error?

entry.title: AVC Procedure Dependency Error When Referencing Variant Table in CU01
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts,

We would like to manage price variants based on intervals defined in a characteristic. For this purpose, we created an AVC variant table in CU60 with two characteristics: ZLARGEUR_NOMINAL and Y_TEST_PROB_CARACT.

However, when we create an AVC procedure dependency in CU01 using the following editor logic:

 
table Z_PRICE_INTERVAL
( ZLARGEUR_NOMINAL = ZLARGEUR_NOMINAL,
Y_TEST_PROB_CARACT = $self.Y_TEST_PROB_CARACT )

the system displays the following error message:

"Unable to process error message."

Could you please advise what might be causing this issue and how to resolve it?

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Auto create Ship to party while creating the sales order
entry.content:
Hi All,

The business requirement is to enable creation of a Ship-To Party directly during sales order creation in SAP SD (S/4HANA) without navigating to the Business Partner (BP) transaction.

A checkbox should be added on the sales order screen, such as “Create Ship-To” or “Save as Ship-To.”

Proposed logic:

The system should first check whether the entered address already exists for the customer.

If the address does not exist, the system should create a new Ship-To Party and automatically assign it to the respective Sold-To Party during sales order creation or when the sales order is saved.

 

Please suggest how to proceed.

Thank you.

entry.title: Sales return to a consignmnet supplier stock
entry.content:
I have stock of a supplier consignment. In the sales outbound delivery post the 601K and consume the supplier consignment stock. This is ok. My problem is when i have a return of this sale, the stock return has own stock, when i need return to the supplier consignment stock. any one knows how can do this?

we dont have the retail solution in the system.

Ty

entry.title: How to Post Tax Amount to Expense G/L When Base Price Is Fully Discounted?
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We have a requirement to post a tax amount to the company’s expense G/L account.

In our scenario, we are giving a product with 100% discount from the base price, but tax should still be calculated on the base price.

Pricing details:

  • PR00 – 100 INR
  • R100 – 100% discount on PR00
  • MWST – 5% calculated on PR00

Currently, this 5% tax amount is being posted to the customer account, but our requirement is to post this tax amount to a company expense G/L account instead.

Could anyone guide me on how to configure the system so that the tax amount posts to a company expense G/L account rather than the customer?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: How to end validity period of alternative bom 1 so I have only active alternative bom 2
entry.content: How to end validity period of alternative bom 1 so I have only active alternative bom 2

entry.title: Review Booklet - Create a Navigation Target
entry.content:
I'm using the SAP Public Cloud. 

In the SAP Public Cloud app "Manage KPIs and Reports," I created a Booklet.
In the configuration for this Booklet, I added two Navigation Targets: one for Sales Orders and the other for Billing Documents. However, when I try to navigate to display a sales order from the respective app, it opens, but the sales order number does not copy and open.

What could be causing this issue? Is there a configuration step that I might have missed?

 

 

I already tried with parameters/value. However, besides the parameter, it is correctly defined, the field where the sales order number should be populated is copied as a "text". 

 

Can someone help me, please?

 
I have attached some screenshots of the process for reference.
 
Thank you.

entry.title: DP90 shows Original Amount but Open Amount = 0 in Resource-Related Billing
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

I am working on a Resource-Related Billing (RRB) scenario and facing an issue during DP90 execution. I would appreciate your guidance.

 

System: SAP S/4HANA

Module involved: SD / PS (Resource-Related Billing)

 

Scenario:

 

1. Costs are posted through MM processes (goods and service procurement).

2. These costs are posted to a WBS element and visible in CJI3 under the relevant cost element.

3. I created a Debit Memo Request (VA01) with a service material.

4. In the sales document item:

   - WBS element is assigned

   - RRB profile is maintained

5. After that, I executed DP90 to transfer the WBS costs for billing.

 

Issue:

 

In the DP90 screen, the system displays the following:

 

- Original Amount: 500,000 USD

- Amount Already Billed: 0

- Open Amount: 0

 

Since the Open Amount is zero, I am unable to generate a billing request or proceed further with billing.

 

Additionally:

 

- I do not see any Dynamic Item Processor (DIP) entries under the Environment menu in VA03 for the Debit Memo Request item.

- The amount appears in the hierarchy in DP90 but seems to be treated as already cancelled or not billable.

 

What I have verified so far:

 

- Costs exist on the WBS element (CJI3).

- Debit Memo Request is created and the WBS element is correctly assigned to the item.

- DIP profile is maintained in the sales document item

 

 

 

 

Questions:

1. What could cause DP90 to show Original Amount but Open Amount = 0?

2. Under what conditions does SAP mark the cost as already cancelled or processed even when billing has not yet been created?

3. Are there specific DIP profile settings or item category settings that could lead to this behavior?

 

Any suggestions on how to troubleshoot or reset the DP90 processing would be greatly appreciated.

 

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: Credit check should not happen again, once Sale Order is released
entry.content:
I am creating a sale Order for quantity 10. But the confirmed quantity is 5. Credit check is happening against the Confirmed quantity 5 and blocking the SO. Then the SO is released using VKM3/VKM4. Then while altering the Quantity above 5 or altering the schedule line date, the order is getting Credit blocked again. This should not happen.

So to avoid this from happening we wanted the Credit check to happen for the Order quantity rather than the Confirmed quantity.

To prevent this we tried altering the LV_AMOUNT in  BADI_SD_CM -> Method FSCM_CREDIT_CHECK_ORDER to take the Total Order amount (Logic=  Net Value + Tax ). Even after this, the old behavior based on Confirmed quantity is happening. Can anyone guide how to achieve the above requirement. Also the Credit exposure for the BP has to be updated based on the Order Quantity.

entry.title: Update Schedule Line Data using SD_SALESDOCUMENT_CHANGE
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am facing an issue when trying to update the schedule line data 
i.e 

I am using SD_SALESDOCUMENT_CHANGE for the changing the schedule lines.
Below is the code i am using 

  ls_header_inx-updateflag = 'U'

  ls_header_in-doc_date    = cs_header-order_date.
  ls_header_inx-doc_date   = abap_true.

  ls_header_in-req_date_h  = cs_header-due_date.
  ls_header_inx-req_date_h = abap_true.

  ls_header_in-ship_cond   = cs_header-trans_type.
  ls_header_inx-ship_cond  = abap_true.

  ls_header_in-purch_no_c  = cs_header-cpo.
  ls_header_inx-purch_no_c = abap_true.

ls_item_inx-updateflag   =  'U'

      ls_item_in-itm_number   = <lfs_vbap>-posnr.
      ls_item_inx-itm_number  = <lfs_vbap>-posnr.

      ls_item_in-target_qty   = <lfs_item>-order_qty.
      ls_item_inx-target_qty  = abap_true.

ls_schedulex-updateflag  = 'U'

        ls_schedule-itm_number   = <lfs_vbep>-posnr.
        ls_schedulex-itm_number  = <lfs_vbep>-posnr.

        ls_schedule-sched_line  = <lfs_vbep>-etenr.
        ls_schedulex-sched_line  = <lfs_vbep>-etenr.

        ls_schedule-req_date    = cs_header-due_date.
        ls_schedulex-req_date   = abap_true.

        ls_schedule-req_qty     = <lfs_item>-order_qty.
        ls_schedulex-req_qty    = abap_true.

         ls_schedule-dlv_date     = <lfs_item>-cs_header-due_date.
        ls_schedulex-dlv_date    = abap_true.

  CALL FUNCTION 'SD_SALESDOCUMENT_CHANGE'
    EXPORTING
      salesdocument     = cs_header-mso
      order_header_in   = ls_header_in
      order_header_inx  = ls_header_inx
*     logic_switch      = ls_logic_switch
    TABLES
      return            = lt_return
      item_in           = lt_item_in
      item_inx          = lt_item_inx
      schedule_in       = lt_schedule
      schedule_inx      = lt_schedulex
      sales_text        = lt_text
    EXCEPTIONS
      incov_not_in_item = 1
      OTHERS            = 2.


Note: I am using bapi_transaction_commit.

The Delivery date in the screenshot is getting updated; but
Goods Issue Date, Loading Date, Material Availability Check and Transportation pland date are not getting updated.

If i pass only schedule line data in the FM using SE37( test sequence ) then the dates are getting updated correctly.
I am doing this in a custom fiori app so the fields i am passing the structures are needed.

entry.title: Copy Control Routine 1
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have created a Quantity Contract and assigned a Release Partner AA and AW.

When creating a Sales Order with reference to the Contract the Bill To and Payer is copied from the Contract whereas it should copy the Bill To and Payer from the Customer Master Partner Function of AA and AW.

I am attaching screenshot of VOPAN and the Copy Control for your reference.

Please can someone please provide me a solution.

This is project critical and we are on RISE .

Thanks and Regards.

entry.title: SO item partner userexit to re-determine other partners
entry.content: User change SO item partner (e.g. Sales Employee 'PE') and we need to re-determine other partner (e.g. End Customer 'EC') on same SO item. Please advise the userexit where we put the ABAP code to other partner re-determination immediately & not in SO saving stage.
